headline: Urban Sprawl Consumes 15,000 Hectares of Farmland Yearly
context: Department of Agriculture data shows 15,000 hectares of productive farmland converted to urban development annually in Peninsula.

cards.big: 15,000 hectares of farmland lost to urban development every year
cards.sub: Food self-sufficiency ratio declined from 65% to 58% between 2020 and 2025

cards.big: Selangor lost 42,000 hectares of rice paddies to housing since 2015
cards.sub: State now produces only 12% of its rice consumption versus 28% a decade ago

cards.big: Planning Act allows state conversion with minimal agricultural impact review
cards.sub: Only 4 of 13 states require food security assessments before land conversion

cards.big: Developers paid RM2.8 billion in conversion premiums to state governments
cards.sub: Revenue incentive means states financially benefit from approving conversion

cards.big: Farmland loss tests whether food security is weighed against development revenue
15,000 hectares of farmland consumed yearly — urban sprawl converts food security into property speculation.
